data.content_ref: inline:{"analogy":{"adopted_commitment_refs":["cca300f0c573cf780a9e03e6e326a28a413e21cf7e7a3d165b20d13788dcbd34"],"disanalogies":["A compiler unifies syntactic and semantic layers by construction, whereas the harness intentionally separates formal adjudication from model semantic content","Static type-checking occurs offline, while harness validation is a runtime state-machine boundary"],"overturn_conditions":["If scheduler school conditioning provably alters route selection outside manifest freeze (C1), the failure becomes a structural route violation","If conj.py actually enforces school-to-route binding via an undocumented constraint, the gap is closed and no failure exists","If the system architecture intends school and route to be orthogonal axes, the observed independence is correct-by-design rather than a gap"],"shared_structure":["Both perform deterministic validation at system boundaries while delegating unbounded semantic content to an external engine","Both enforce a strict wire contract between the deterministic layer and the open-ended engine","Both rely on replayable manifest artifacts to reproduce historical execution"],"source_memory_refs":["1044806d358e10c7e52f5170028eba84ae105a91d1700653c293d3decb391853","cca300f0c573cf780a9e03e6e326a28a413e21cf7e7a3d165b20d13788dcbd34"],"transfer_claims":["The harness should expose a deterministic SchoolBindingPolicy step between scheduling and adapter lease acquisition to close the orthogonal-axes gap without restricting model content","The compiler's separation of AST validation from semantic evaluation validates the harness's separation of formal state adjudication from scratch content"]},"claim":"The actual present failure is an incomplete orthogonal-axes architecture: the harness enforces C1/C5 (immutable routes, prose cannot control flow) at the route-lease and adapter-wire-contract layer, but the scheduler iterates schools as prompt/provenance conditioning (scheduler.py:410-520) without binding each school to a distinct configured route. This creates a prompt-delegation gap where schools are semantically conditioned but structurally unconstrained in route selection. The incorrectly prompt-delegated behaviour is the assumption that school identity and route identity are coupled by convention rather than enforced by manifest freeze. The correctly harness-owned behaviours are: manifest route freeze (run_manifest.py:412-500), append-only event commit (harness.py:180-280), formal/scratch/bridge state separation (harness.py:69-116), and budget reservation enforcement (budget.py:79-158). The strongest deterministic control architecture treats the harness as a two-pass compiler: the first pass resolves and freezes all control-flow bindings (routes, budgets, phases, school-to-route maps) into an immutable execution manifest; the second pass executes model calls strictly within those frozen bindings, where each model call is a pure content function whose output is adjudicated by the harness state machine.","counterconditions":[{"case":"If scheduler school conditioning is intentionally orthogonal to route selection by design, the gap is not a failure but a deliberate decoupling preserving model freedom","eval":"observation"},{"case":"If a future requirement emerges for school-specific routing, the manifest must absorb this as a frozen binding rather than a runtime negotiation","eval":"observation"},{"case":"If the harness already validates school-route compatibility at a layer not captured in the observed code, this conjecture is falsified by missing evidence","eval":"observation"}],"definitions":[],"derivation":[],"formalization_refs":[],"mechanism":"Deterministic components: (1) Manifest freeze locks role routes and v3 scratch/bridge policies (run_manifest.py:412-500); (2) conflict-safe run-root binding to exactly one canonical manifest (run_manifest.py:1328-1406); (3) registration validates canonical interfaces and commits append-only events (harness.py:180-280); (4) formal/scratch/bridge states replayed into separate materialized structures, only formal state enters adjudication (harness.py:69-116); (5) stopping eligibility and escape-ladder in deterministic software state (stop.py:1-180); (6) continuation verifies manifest identity before appending (continuation.py:1-190); (7) attention selection and coverage receipts deterministically planned and appended at state fence (scratch/attention.py:405-469). Model-call components: (1) adapter verifies route leases and rejects mechanism drift (adapter.py:300-430); (2) budget reservations enforced before dispatch (budget.py:79-158); (3) scratch authoring offers one bounded model task over committed advisory context (scratch/authoring.py:27-119); (4) bridge runs typed two-stage workflow asserting formal state unchanged (bridge/harness.py:286-457). Typed interfaces: SchoolBindingPolicy{school_id, route_id, budget_lease} -> FrozenBinding; ModelCall{input_context, wire_contract, frozen_binding} -> ModelResult{typed_content}; Adjudication{model_result, formal_state} -> StatusEffect. Transition semantics: harness transitions are append-only events; model calls are side-effect-free content functions; formal state changes only through adjudication; scratch is non-authoritative (C8). Manifest/events/replay: all consequential transitions logged (C2); replay produces identical formal state (C3); manifest freezes routes and policies (C1). Evidence and security: evidence acquisition distinct from reasoning (C7); bridge assembles evidence separately; adapter rejects mechanism drift. Migration: introduce SchoolBindingPolicy validation step between scheduler iteration and adapter lease acquisition; freeze school-to-route bindings in manifest; no change to model output content or reasoning freedom. Retained open-ended freedom: model produces any content within typed responsibility (C4, C10); harness adjudicates independently (C9). Strongest likely failure mode: a future model-call site bypasses SchoolBindingPolicy validation and acquires a route lease inconsistent with its school conditioning, creating an unlogged semantic-control coupling. Falsifying tests: assert every model call site acquires its route lease through a SchoolBindingPolicy-validated path; assert manifest freeze includes school-to-route bindings; assert replay produces identical formal state with school conditioning stubbed; assert scheduler cannot produce a route lease without manifest-frozen binding; assert criticism prose has no formal status effect in the event log.","premises":[],"schema":"deepreason-reasoning-envelope-v1","scope":{"covers":[],"excludes":[]},"simulation_refs":[],"uncertainties":[]}
